# UC-18.4.5 · NX-OS VXLAN EVPN Fabric Underlay BGP Health

## Description

Cisco Nexus 9000 NX-OS 10.x VXLAN EVPN spine-leaf Clos fabrics anchor tenant reachability on BGP for both underlay control planes (iBGP, OSPF area 0 backbone paths, IS-IS Level-2 PE-CE alternatives) and BGP-EVPN L2VPN EVPN overlays with RD formats such as auto:ASN:VNI, route-target auto semantics, fabric forwarding mode anycast-gateway-mac, loopback-1 VTEP sourcing, vPC peer-link resilience tied to peer-keepalive, EVPN NLRI Types 1–5, NVE peer states (UP, DOWN, DELAY-RESPONSE, SUPPRESS-FLOOD), evi mappings such as evi 10001, syslog MNEMONICs BGP-5-ADJCHANGE, BGP_NBR_DOWN/UP, OSPF-5-ADJCHG, IS_IS-5-ADJCHANGE, NVE-3-PEER_DOWN, L2RIB-3-EVPN_TYPE2_DUPMAC, parity checks via NX-API plus show bgp l2vpn evpn summary / show nve peers / show ip ospf neighbor, Splunkbase 7777 fabric rollups, Nexus Dashboard Insights /sedgeapi/v1/site_health JSON, and NDFC inventory pulls — distinct from generic overlays because remediation routes through NX-OS native nve/l2rib/vpc/bfd instrumentation.

## Value

When **BGP** underlay sessions oscillate or stick short of **Established**, **VTEP** reachability contracts and **EVPN** advertisements wobble before tenants see brownouts. **Splunk** joins **NX-OS** neighbors, **NVE** peers, EVPN prefix counters, and **NDI** **site_health** posture so spine work, ISSU freezes, and controller baselines stay interpretable—shortening incident **MTTR** while making **MTBF** regressions visible to platform owners.

## Implementation

**Quick win:** ship **Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus** (**6876**) **syslog** plus **NX-API** snapshots into **`cisco:nxos:bgp`** and **`cisco:nxos:nve`** before adding **`cisco:ndi:health`**—you get flap and tunnel evidence in one sprint without a custom collector program.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus (Splunkbase 6876) on Heavy Forwarders and Search Heads so facility local7 syslog retains Cisco MNEMONIC text for BGP, OSPF, IS-IS, NVE, and L2RIB narratives routed to **cisco:nxos:syslog** without stripping tokens Splunk macros rely upon later.
• Schedule HTTPS collectors (Heavy Forwarder identities or scripted inputs) hitting NX-API **/api/mo/sys/bgp.json**, **/api/mo/sys/nve.json**, and **/api/mo/sys/ospf.json** every 60 seconds—stamp **cisco:nxos:bgp**, **cisco:nxos:nve**, **cisco:nxos:ospf** respectively and backoff on HTTP 429 responses per Cisco automation guidance.
• Configure rest_ta (Splunkbase 1546) against **https://<nd-cluster>/appcenter/cisco/ndfc/api/v1/lan-fabric/rest/control/fabrics/{fabric}/inventory** with Nexus Dashboard bearer tokens stored beside **passwords.conf** rotations—landing **cisco:ndfc:fabric** snapshots used only for ISSU and inventory drift correlation, not as a substitute for switch-local BGP tables.
• Poll **https://<nd-cluster>/sedgeapi/v1/site_health** into **cisco:ndi:health** JSON keyed by **siteName** so Splunk overlays controller posture without importing **cisco:ndi:anomaly** feeds emphasized under sibling dashboards.
• Deploy cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk (Splunkbase 7777) so packaged tiles consume the same sourcetype spellings referenced throughout this UC—rename macros instead of sourcetypes once TA mappings stabilize.
• Optional gNMI receivers streaming **network-instance/protocols/bgp/neighbors** plus **nv overlay** telemetry dedupe against syslog via **host** plus **mgmt0** fingerprints to prevent double-counting adjacency churn during syslog HA relays.

Step 1 — Data sources
Blend planes deliberately—(a) **cisco:nxos:syslog** carries **BGP-5-ADJCHANGE**, **BGP_NBR_DOWN**, **BGP_NBR_UP**, **OSPF-5-ADJCHG**, **IS_IS-5-ADJCHANGE**, **NVE-3-PEER_DOWN**, **L2RIB-3-EVPN_TYPE2_DUPMAC** strings tied to anycast-gateway-mac, loopback-1, peer-keepalive, and evi constructs—(b) **cisco:nxos:bgp** exposes **bgp_peer_ip**, **bgp_peer_asn**, **bgp_state** (Idle through Established), **bgp_uptime**, and **peer_role** (underlay-spine, underlay-leaf, overlay-rr)—(c) **cisco:nxos:nve** lists **nve_peer_ip**, **nve_peer_state**, **vni_id**, counters comparable to **show nve peers**—(d) **cisco:ndi:health** merges **fabricScore** posture JSON—(e) **cisco:ndfc:fabric** captures ISSU queues and assurance deltas—(f) optional gNMI pushes interface discard counters alongside BGP churn so dataplane drops surface beside control-plane timelines on Nexus 93180YC-FX, 9332C, 9504, and 9508 roles commonly deployed as leaf/spine pairs.

Step 2 — Field extractions
Normalize once—(1) **props.conf** extracts **bgp_peer_ip** using neighbor regex anchors aligned with syslog plus REST payloads—(2) **transforms.conf** aliases **peer_role** enumerations emitted differently across syslog versus REST—(3) unify **nve_peer_state** lexical variants (UP, DOWN, DELAY-RESPONSE, SUPPRESS-FLOOD) before KPI joins—(4) populate **l2vpn_evpn_routes** integers referencing **show bgp l2vpn evpn summary** totals split conceptually across Type-1, Type-2, Type-3, Type-4, and Type-5 buckets—(5) capture **ospf_neighbor_state** + **ospf_area** beside **loopback-1** references—(6) capture **isis_adjacency_state** when IS-IS participates—(7) normalize **route_distinguisher** strings honoring **auto:ASN:VNI** semantics plus **route_target** auto symmetry checks—(8) maintain **mac_mobility_count** counters correlating **L2RIB-3-EVPN_TYPE2_DUPMAC** bursts—(9) ingest **vpc_peer_keepalive** fields separating peer-link loss from isolated NVE faults.

Step 3 — Initial validation
Splunk rows must reconcile with CLI truth—(a) compare **bgp_state** timelines against **show bgp l2vpn evpn summary** screenshots archived per incident—(b) validate **nve_peer_state** versus **show nve peers** Peer-state referencing loopback-1 sources—(c) confirm **fabricScore** trends align with Nexus Dashboard Insights widgets excluding anomaly-category feeds—(d) compare **cisco:ndfc:fabric** assurance grades against **show consistency-check** captures gathered during readiness drills—(e) run Cisco-documented VXLAN convergence exercises so Splunk fingerprints mirror scripted faults—(f) verify **peer-keepalive** stays healthy whenever **vPC** **peer-link** syslog arcs flap—(g) capture **show ip ospf neighbor** FULL counts beside Splunk **ospf_neighbor_state** distributions—(h) retain **show isis adjacency** snapshots when dual-stack fabrics rely on wide metrics—(i) prove **fabric forwarding mode anycast-gateway-mac** contexts remain steady across **evi** attachments referenced inside **route_distinguisher** tuples.

Step 4 — SPL building blocks
Implement staged macros feeding alerts—(1) **macro:bgp_underlay_flap_pair** counts **BGP-5-ADJCHANGE** events span=1m grouped by **host**, **bgp_peer_ip**, **peer_role** highlighting spine ↔ leaf churn thresholds—(2) **macro:nve_peer_down_storm** tracks **NVE-3-PEER_DOWN** bursts plus **DELAY-RESPONSE**/SUPPRESS-FLOOD transitions signaling dataplane contention—(3) **macro:evpn_route_count_drop** contrasts **l2vpn_evpn_routes** deltas versus **predict** baselines catching Type-5 withdrawals unrelated to ISSU freezes—(4) **macro:rt_auto_leak** searches syslog for **route-target** auto asymmetry leaking foreign **vn-segment** advertisements—(5) **macro:type2_dup_mac_surge** escalates **L2RIB-3-EVPN_TYPE2_DUPMAC** frequency crossing **mac_mobility_count** budgets—(6) **macro:ospf_cost_asymm** contrasts **ospf_neighbor_state** stability with **interface** cost deltas pinpointing area 0 pinning—(7) **macro:isis_level2_drop** captures IS-IS regressions neighboring BGP churn—(8) **macro:vpc_peer_keepalive_gap** correlates **vpc** syslog arcs with **nve** regressions distinguishing ISSU **reload** storms—(9) **macro:ndi_health_bridge** overlays **cisco:ndi:health** deterioration atop BGP/NVE timelines—(10) **macro:data_plane_drop_corr** merges Ethernet **drop** counters streamed via gNMI—(11) **macro:bgp_bfd_disagree** contrasts BFD session loss markers against lingering BGP Established states exposing stale best-path hazards.

Step 5 — Comprehensive enrichment patterns
Before paging escalates—(a) merge **cisco:ndi:health** **fabricSite** posture scores with Splunk site lookups so MTTR narratives cite site_health dips minus anomaly-category telemetry—(b) nightly cisco:ndfc:fabric snapshots establish fabric assurance baselines; Splunk diff macros highlight inventory churn preceding BGP regressions—(c) ISSU orchestration contexts downgrade benign Graceful Restart windows yet escalate stuck reload queues threatening loopback-1 continuity—(d) schedule Cisco openVuln pulls tagging CSCvw defects impacting BGP-EVPN control planes—cross-match Bug-ID tokens from show version syslog—(e) embed ServiceNow INC references plus Cisco TAC SR bridges whenever ndi_health collapses coincide with BGP flap KPI breaches—(f) optional Splunk ITSI layers tenant identifiers onto VNI/evi tuples so business stakeholders view instability through contracted services rather than ASN-only math—(g) compare Type-2 MAC/IP advertisement volumes against documented hardware ECMP ceilings maintained by platform architects—(h) correlate NX-OS interface discard telemetry (InDiscards, OutDiscards, CRC) streamed via gNMI adjacent to NVE storms—(i) compare BFD sessions anchored on loopback underlays with BGP fast-external-failover posture highlighting timer drift versus orchestration guides—(j) isolate route_distinguisher auto:ASN:VNI mismatches via bgp_l2vpn_evpn RD formatter outputs—(k) augment narratives with show vpc Peer status datapoints whenever peer-keepalive symmetry slips—(l) differentiate dup-mac bursts tied to workload migrations versus bridging faults using hypervisor schedules—(m) replay CHG tickets documenting purposeful OSPF metric edits through macro:ospf_cost_asymm—(n) observe IS-IS metric widen events mirrored alongside BGP churn inside dual-stack fabrics—(o) annotate Splunkbase 7777 dashboards with BGP/EVPN overlays referencing site_health widgets—(p) tune predict outlier thresholds referencing golden Saturday snapshots unaffected by weekday tenant onboarding bursts—(q) translate fabric names into on-call bridges via lookups accelerating Sev-1 rotations—(r) append CVE tokens lifted from openVuln responses into pager payloads satisfying security reviewers—(s) document drift baseline resets post-ISSU beside ndi_health replay pointers preserving auditor narratives—(t) suppress purposeful BGP route-refresh spikes following route-map edits using CHG macros referencing NDFC deploy batches—(u) optionally chain Enterprise Security risk modifiers when fabric tier classifications demand SOC visibility adjacent to NetOps paging lanes.

Step 6 — Operational runbook
Run responders calmly—(1) declare BGP-down Sev-1 paging when macro:bgp_underlay_flap_pair clears rehearsal thresholds concurrently with ndi_health deterioration—(2) engage Cisco TAC when openVuln references intersect embargoed CSCvw defects affecting deployed NX-OS trains—(3) publish fabric-tier MTBF targets (example ≥180 days between flap storms) beside MTTR objectives (example ≤15 minutes from Splunk detection to mitigation)—(4) execute drift-baseline resets after ISSU by rerunning predict training and archiving CSV exports proving baseline renewal—(5) satisfy PCI/SOX reviewers by retaining nightly CSV snapshots inside restricted indexes referencing BGP/EVPN observability packs—(6) rehearse nve storms paired with vpc peer-link failover choreography quarterly—(7) suppress benign pager noise during documented ISSU calendars yet escalate unfinished reload tasks breaching SLA guardrails—(8) cite Splunk dashboards (BGP peer heatmap, NVE matrix, EVPN route trend, MAC mobility timeline, OSPF/IS-IS stability) inside post-incident templates mandated by leadership—(9) clarify NetOps versus virtualization ownership whenever dup-mac narratives intersect mobility teams—(10) hyperlink RFC7432, RFC8365, and Cisco VXLAN configuration guides inside onboarding notebooks engineers inherit via rotations.

Promotion tip — paste the spl JSON body into Savedsearches.conf after validating fields using head probes; schedule five-minute cadence on critical fabrics and dedup bursts keyed on host, bgp_peer_ip, minute_bucket to suppress syslog retries.


## SPL

```spl
(index=cisco_dc OR index=cisco_network OR index=network_operations OR index=network)
(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:bgp" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:nve" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndi:health")
earliest=-4h@m
| rex field=_raw "(?i)neighbor\s+(?<rex_peer>\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{1,3}){3})"
| eval bgp_peer_ip=trim(coalesce(bgp_peer_ip, neighbor_ip, bgp_neighbor, peer_ipv4, peer_ip, rex_peer))
| eval bgp_state=upper(trim(coalesce(bgp_state, neighbor_state, bgp_fsm_state, session_state)))
| eval peer_role=lower(trim(coalesce(peer_role, rr_role, topo_role, fabric_role)))
| eval nve_peer_state=lower(trim(coalesce(nve_peer_state, peer_state_nve, nve_oper_state)))
| eval l2vpn_evpn_routes=tonumber(coalesce(l2vpn_evpn_routes, evpn_route_total, bgp_evpn_prefixes, evpn_paths))
| eval ndi_site_score=tonumber(coalesce(site_health_score, fabricScore, siteScore, health_score))
| eval bgp_flap=case(
    sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" AND match(_raw,"BGP-5-ADJCHANGE|BGP_NBR_DOWN|BGP_NBR_UP"), 1,
    sourcetype="cisco:nxos:bgp" AND bgp_state!="ESTABLISHED", 1,
    0)
| eval nve_storm=case(
    match(nve_peer_state,"down|inactive|delay-response|suppress-flood"), 1,
    match(lower(_raw),"NVE-3-PEER_DOWN"), 1,
    0)
| eval rt_leak=if(match(lower(_raw),"route.?target|rt.?import|rt.?export") AND match(lower(_raw),"mismatch|unexpected|foreign"), 1, 0)
| eval route_drop_anomaly=if(isnotnull(evpn_route_baseline) AND isnotnull(l2vpn_evpn_routes) AND l2vpn_evpn_routes < tonumber(evpn_route_baseline), 1, 0)
| eval ndi_degraded=if(isnotnull(ndi_site_score) AND ndi_site_score < 70, 1, 0)
| bin span=1m _time as minute_bucket
| stats sum(bgp_flap) as flap_cnt,
        sum(nve_storm) as nve_storm_cnt,
        sum(rt_leak) as rt_leak_cnt,
        sum(route_drop_anomaly) as route_drop_cnt,
        max(ndi_degraded) as ndi_bad,
        latest(l2vpn_evpn_routes) as evpn_routes_latest,
        latest(bgp_state) as bgp_state_now,
        latest(nve_peer_state) as nve_state_now,
        latest(peer_role) as peer_role_now
    by minute_bucket, host, bgp_peer_ip
| eval peer_pair_key=host."|".if(isnull(bgp_peer_ip) OR bgp_peer_ip="","unknown",bgp_peer_ip)
| where flap_cnt>=3 OR nve_storm_cnt>=2 OR rt_leak_cnt>=1 OR route_drop_cnt>=1 OR ndi_bad=1
| eval alert_lane=case(
    flap_cnt>=5 AND peer_role_now IN ("underlay-leaf","leaf"), "bgp_underlay_flap_pair",
    nve_storm_cnt>=3, "nve_peer_storm",
    rt_leak_cnt>=1, "rt_misconfig_leak",
    route_drop_cnt>=1, "evpn_route_count_drop",
    ndi_bad=1, "ndi_health_bridge",
    1=1, "composite_review")
| eval fabric_story="BGP=".bgp_state_now." NVE=".nve_state_now." EVPN=".evpn_routes_latest." ROLE=".peer_role_now
| sort - flap_cnt - nve_storm_cnt
| head 400
| table minute_bucket host bgp_peer_ip peer_pair_key flap_cnt nve_storm_cnt rt_leak_cnt route_drop_cnt ndi_bad alert_lane fabric_story
```

## Visualization

(1) **Heatmap** — **`bgp_state`** × **`peer_role`** segmented by **`fabric`** / **`site`**. (2) **Matrix** — **`nve_peer_state`** across spine/leaf pairs highlighting **`DELAY-RESPONSE`** and **`SUPPRESS-FLOOD`**. (3) **Timechart** — **`l2vpn_evpn_routes`** per **`route_distinguisher`** / **`evi`** with **`predict`** overlays. (4) **Timeline** — **`L2RIB-3-EVPN_TYPE2_DUPMAC`** bursts vs **`mac_mobility_count`**. (5) **Dual-axis chart** — **`OSPF`** FULL stability vs **`IS-IS`** UP adjacency counts bucketed per minute.

- [IETF RFC 8365 — Network Virtualization Overlay Solution Using EVPN](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8365)
